1 repas par jour pour un élève: 1 meal a day for a student
100% de votre don est dédié aux élèves: 100% of your donation goes to students
Atelier de nail art à l'école de Fatima,: Nail art workshop at Fatima's school, supervised by professionals. A moment of creativity and training for our students.
Aujourd'hui, l'association accueille plu: Today, the association welcomes more than 130 students every year, young people aged 11 to 18 from disadvantaged backgrounds, supervised by 15 teachers. Under the presidency of Pauline Martin and the direction of Maryline Lamy, more than 2,000 students have found their way back to confidence and success.
Captage d'eau de pluie et installation d: Rainwater harvesting and installation of a drinking water fountain
Captage de l'eau de pluie et installatio: Rainwater harvesting and installation of a drinking water fountain in the schoolyard, providing free and safe access to water for all pupils and staff. This project improves hygiene conditions and daily well-being.
Chaque jour, 130 élèves trouvent ici un: Every day, 130 students find here a place of learning, respect and hope.
Création d'un jardin écologique au sein: Creation of an ecological garden within the school where pupils learn about composting, vegetable growing and respect for the environment. A living educational tool that raises awareness of nature.
Depuis 2000, un programme pratique pour:
Since 2000, a practical program for teenagers with activities such as massage, hairdressing, beauty care, cooking, computer skills, carpentry, flower arranging, etc.
The aim: employability and independence.
Depuis 37 ans, nous aidons des enfants d: For 37 years, we have been helping underprivileged children in the north of Mauritius to overcome academic failure. More than 2,000 pupils have found their way back to confidence, self-esteem and success.
For 37 years, we have been helping underprivileged children escape from academic failure. More than 2,000 pupils have found their way back to confidence, self-esteem and success.
Des initiatives concrètes pour améliorer: Concrete initiatives to improve the daily lives of our students and preserve our environment.
Découvrez le quotidien de nos élèves et: Find out more about our students' daily lives and the highlights of community life.
En 1989, Madame Janine Grant et un group: In 1989, Mrs. Janine Grant and a group of volunteers from the north of Mauritius realized that many children in the village of Trou-aux-Biches were not attending school or were severely behind academically. They opened a small reading workshop in the courtyard of the parish of Notre Dame de Fatima, with just 14 pupils.
Ensemble, donnons à ces enfants une chan: Together, let's give these children a chance to succeed!
Fontaine d'eau filtrée potable, installé: Drinking filtered water fountain, installed in the school playground
Grâce à un accompagnement basé sur l'amo: Thanks to support based on love, respect and valuing each individual, more than 2,000 students have found their way back to confidence, self-esteem and success.
The year is off to a flying start. Every day at 10:35, the students gather for a moment of prayer, sharing and reminding themselves of the school's values.
La Fête de l'Indépendance a donné lieu c: This year's Independence Day was once again marked by a beautiful show of dance, music and poetry, led by our dedicated teachers!
Le bâtiment de l'école dans la cour de l: The school building in the Fatima parish courtyard
Les fondations. Les enfants déscolarisés: The foundations. Out-of-school children learn to read, write and count in a caring environment. The aim: to make up for lost time and restore confidence.
Les élèves approfondissent leurs compéte: Students deepen their academic skills to take the Certificate of Primary Education (PSAC) and enter the mainstream school system.
MCB Juice, MCB Mobile, ou votre banque h: MCB Juice, MCB Mobile, or your usual bank
Mise en place de stations de tri sélecti: Selective sorting stations are set up in the school and pupils are trained in eco-responsible gestures. Children become ambassadors for recycling in their community.
Natation, Musique, Chant, Danse, Footbal: Swimming, Music, Singing, Dancing, Football, Creative Arts...etc.
Nos élèves (15-18 ans) participent au \": Our students (aged 15-18) take part in Reef Conservation's "Club Mer": snorkeling, first aid and marine ecology.
Notre objectif : les aider à progresser,: Our aim is to help them progress, blossom and become tomorrow's citizens in a welcoming environment adapted to their needs, pace and reality.
Notre école ne classe pas les élèves par: Our school doesn't classify pupils by age, but by skill level. Divided into 5 levels, each child progresses at his or her own pace towards mastery of reading, writing and arithmetic.
Pour poursuivre cette belle mission, nou: To continue this wonderful mission, we need YOU! Young people aged 11 to 18 from disadvantaged backgrounds are counting on your support.

Trois musiciens du prestigieux Philharmo: Three musicians from the prestigious Philharmonia Orchestra visited our students during their tour of Mauritius. An enriching musical encounter.
Un chemin progressif adapté au rythme de: A progressive path adapted to each child's pace, from literacy to professional integration.
Une ONG dédiée à l'éducation et à l'inté: An NGO dedicated to the education and social integration of vulnerable children in the north of Mauritius.
